paragraph: Single-family homes in Norwood are moving at a median price of $916,550 with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.99 and a median of 25.5 days on market. About 41 percent of homes sold above asking, which means priced homes are moving and sellers aren't necessarily waiting months for the perfect offer. The spread between median and average sale price tells you the market includes everything from $600,000 starter homes to high-end properties over $3.8 million, so your competition depends heavily on where you're looking within that range. Condos and townhouses are smaller in volume with 12 sales over eight months at a median of $737,500, slightly above the median list price and a full 1.0 sale-to-list ratio. Median days on market sits at 32.5, a touch slower than single-family, which suggests these units require a bit more patience to sell even when priced fairly. If you're deciding between tiers, the single-family market offers more inventory and tighter timing, while condos give you a lower entry point and the option of a smaller footprint.
